response:
A rectangle has side lengths in the ratio of $2:5$. If all of its sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the resulting rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $3:7$. 

*highlighted section* The original rectangle has side lengths in the ratio $2:5$, which means if the shorter side is $2x$, the longer side is $5x$. When all sides are extended by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. 

*highlighted section* The new ratio is given as $3:7$, so we can set up the equation:  
$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$  
Cross-multiplying gives:  
$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9)
$$  
$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$  
$$
63 - 27 = 15x - 14x
$$  
$$
36 = x
$$  

*highlighted section* Substituting $x = 36$ back into the original side lengths:  
- Shorter side: $2x = 72 \text{ cm}$  
- Longer side: $5x = 180 \text{ cm}$  

*highlighted section* When all sides are extended by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become:  
- Shorter side: $72 + 9 = 81 \text{ cm}$  
- Longer side: $180 + 9 = 189 \text{ cm}$  

*highlighted section* The new ratio is:  
$$
\frac{81}{189} = \frac{3}{7}
$$  
This confirms the calculation is correct.  

*highlighted section* The ratio of the side lengths of the rectangle that results from extending all of its sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$ is $3:7$.
